reviews.review_description: I play this game with some friends and we have a lot of fun. It’s quite unique as you sabotage the other players using creative items and elements as they are trying to get through the course. Players can play on mobile or in VR. I recommend this game if you are looking for a new twist on golf.

I love this game.  It can be played locally and remotely.  This matters a lot to me when I want to do something inclusive with my family at home while including out of town family.  What I especially like, at home, is that we can rotate the headset use and put everyone else on the app to sabotage as little or as much as they'd like.  There's not a lot of games out there that can be this accommodating for organized groups of friends and family.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review, intjmega! This is exactly the scenario we imagined when we dreamed up Fruit Golf. 🥹 Thanks a bunch for playing, and please let us know if we can ever help or if you have suggestions to make your golfing experience better! (support@fruitgolf.com)

reviews.review_title: Just give it a go mate
reviews.review_description:
I was a tester
Really a great game, I’ve always found that most vr golf games just feel off… you get me? It never feels like what I’m doing affects my ball enough. This game nails it. Different balls really do affect the physics, it’s great. The asymmetrical aspect of having one of your friends giggling as they throw a shipwreck at you or a Minecraft truely isn’t available anywhere else. Great game, love to see it.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much, SOZ.strikess! ⛳🍌 We’re glad the physics feel right and that the asymmetrical chaos hit the mark. Friends giggling while sabotaging your shot is what Fruit Golf is all about.

reviews.review_description:
Its basically mini golf with a lot of chaotic energy with stuff flying around to obstruct or troll you. You have to play with other people who can just throw things at you, its not the same fun alone when stuff falls randomly.
Its not just obstructions, some items have interesting behavior, like portals to bring that golf ball way back (will make the golfer mad :] )
The Environments are also nice and actually have unique mechanics and also dictate what items the others get to make them fit the map.
You could also play as an obstructor with the phone app if you want to play locally or with friends without headsets, but everyone playing in VR gives the best experience

developer_response.body: Hi gWinnieVR! Thanks for the review! 🍌 Fruit Golf is absolutely better when friends are there to troll you with portals, hazards, and flying nonsense. Glad you enjoyed the chaos! Please let us know if there's more we can do to improve single-player mode!

reviews.review_description:
There are not enough games that combine VR with nonVR players. I have multiple VR devices in my home, so getting everyone to play a game together can be tough. Fortunately for nonVR players, they can use their phone or tablet to play the free mobile version. On Android devices, we had zero problems. My wife, who hates VR, actually gets to interact with everyone else playing.
The VR player races to the end of the course while the non-VR player pummels them with traps and hazards. We haven’t laughed this hard as a family in a long time! Getting set up is super easy and fast. The courses are colorful and fun, and there are plenty of unlockables. The main downside is that it is not meant to be played solo; you really need to have multiple players on board who each own a copy of the game.

developer_response.body: This made our day! Thank you for playing! We’re so happy Fruit Golf helped your family play together across VR and mobile, especially with someone who normally avoids VR. That shared chaos is exactly what we hoped players would enjoy. <3

reviews.review_description:
This is the goofiest game of mini-golf you may ever play. Hitting living fruits with things like a rose club, a sunflower or several other random things. The courses are over the top distracting and sometimes frustrating. Meanwhile, you can have 3 other players just dropping random obstacles in your path making it even more challenging, maybe even more frustrating at times. Playing alone is kind of boring, as the NPC saboteurs are slow and not as calculated as some of your friends might be. 
With the friends though, you have to have some, because you aren’t finding a game of random people to play with. You have to have a host and room code if you want multiple players. However, this is pretty fun for a gathering of friends as others can join on their mobile devices to be the ones dropping the obstacles on you. It’s not as fun doing it in VR, but it can get others involved. 
The movement system is a bit clunky feeling as default is snap turn and teleport and instant teleport to your ball, unless a specific distraction is dropped, then it might not be your ball at all. But, even with smooth turn enabled, it is so slow that you are better off just physically moving on your own, provided you have the space. Overall though, it is a fun game. There are a lot of things to unlock, despite them not being so clear as to how to unlock them. Your first few games will feel like you are getting a reward every time, whether a new club, map or outfit. So, playing alone to unlock things isn’t such a bad idea when your friends do finally try it out and they have choices to hit a lemon with a rose or a watermelon with a sheriff club.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the thoughtful review, Satconcook! We’re glad the goofy chaos came through, especially with friends jumping in on mobile as saboteurs. We really appreciate the notes on solo play, movement, and unlock clarity, too. That feedback is genuinely helpful as we keep improving the experience. Hope your friends enjoy their first round of fruit-based mini golf mayhem!

reviews.review_description:
The game combines classic mini-golf mechanics with exaggerated obstacles, fruit-themed environments, and a progression system that keeps things moving at a comfortable pace.  Obstacles are dropped by the other players.

Matches are fast, which makes it ideal for short gaming sessions.

Visually, the game embraces a bright cartoon aesthetic. The environments are cheerful and varied enough to keep the presentation entertaining without becoming distracting. 

It’s an enjoyable and entertaining experience for casual groups looking to laugh and compete together. Even if you don’t have a headset.

developer_response.body:
Hi Powergirl! We are so happy to see your name in our reviews! 

Thank you so much for your extensive feedback; it really helped the team, and for your spectacular review! You're a real peach!

reviews.review_title: A Hilarious Twist on Mini Golf
reviews.review_description:
This game is pure chaos and I mean that in the best way possible.
Picture a calm round of mini golf, then add fruit headed characters, ridiculous outfits, sabotage mechanics, and a whole lot of competitive energy. That’s the experience. It starts light and silly, but the second someone lines up their shot, everything explodes into laughter and yelling.

The customization is half the fun. You’ve got fruit heads, unlockable outfits, and unlockable golf clubs. You do not just look random, you look intentionally ridiculous. Even the golf balls are fruit, which somehow makes every missed shot feel even more dramatic. Everyone ends up choosing something that fits their personality, and that makes the competition feel weirdly personal in the funniest way.

But the real twist? No one politely waits their turn.

While one player is putting, everyone else is actively trying to sabotage them. Obstacles pop up, distractions fly in, and that peaceful little putt becomes a full survival challenge. You can swing your club to clear things out, but you have to react fast. Every hole turns into a frantic scramble where you are half focused, half panicking, and fully laughing.

There are multiple courses to unlock so it stays fresh, and multiplayer supports up to four players. Once the energy ramps up, the room fills with competitive chaos, loud reactions, playful blaming, and over the top celebrations when someone pulls off an impossible shot.

If you only have one VR headset, everyone else can jump in using their phones. It becomes a true group game instead of a pass the headset situation. No one is sitting out. Everyone plays at the same time.

It is currently in Early Access, so you may run into small bugs, but nothing that ruins the experience. The foundation is strong and already delivers a ton of fun. 

If you want calm, relaxing golf, this is not it.
If you want loud, chaotic, competitive fun with family/friends, this is absolutely worth it. Must have in your game library.

developer_response.body: Hiya, KRGoldy! Thanks for the thoughtful review and for embracing the chaos! Our art team had a lot of fun designing the different cosmetics, and it brings us so much joy to hear that you enjoyed checking out our different avatars and clubs. We really wanted Fruit Golf to be an accessible and inclusive VR experience, and we appreciate you calling out that no one gets left out, even if you only have access to one headset. Allowing players to sabotage golfers from their mobile device is something we’re super proud of. We’re always looking for ways to improve Fruit Golf, and we ap-peach-iciate you sharing your thoughts and feedback. We hope you continue to swing into the chaos!

reviews.review_title: Fruit Frenzy: A berry good time in VR!
reviews.review_description:
This game had me laughing so hard my cheeks hurt... and my hands a little too from all the gripping. Totally worth it! It is pure chaotic joy with other players conspiring against you at every turn. While it sounds heart-racing and may not be for someone looking for a slower-paced game, I mainly play VR for therapeutic and often meditative purposes... yet games like these become rare staples when the laughs are endless.

My absolute favorite part is that the fruit balls wail, whimper, and whine like tiny little divas and I could not stop belly laughing every time I hit one or even just approached one. The pomegranate is my absolute favorite ball. From first launch, I felt like a big kid again, but it never felt like a game just for kids. It somehow brings out your inner child while keeping the gameplay smart and family friendly.

A safe, spacious play area with a rotating chair makes aligning with your ball much easier as the golfer, especially if you have mobility limits. Saboteuring requires endless strategy and keeps your arms busy while golfing is a mix of quickness, panic, and hilarious outcomes. The variety of obstacles, mini-world environments, and outrageous interactions keeps every round fresh and unpredictable.

The game is still in early access, so there are some quirks being ironed out. We had occasional audio issues that usually fixed themselves by exiting and re-entering the room. None of the issues I experienced stopped me from starting another game until my body reminded me I needed to eat and my hands needed a break. Quirks aside, it is endlessly fun, affordable, and unforgettable. I give it 5 stars for pure, chaotic fun and laughter and I will be coming back for more mayhem as soon as I can.

The accompanying mobile app is a fantastic bonus, letting friends and family join in the chaos from anywhere in the world with a simple room code. Mobile players cannot golf, but they can play saboteur and become a real menace, placing obstacles to boost the golfer’s stroke count. It adds even more laughter and strategy to the mix. Mobile players also have a microphone, so it truly feels like everyone is playing together in VR.

developer_response.body:
Hi muggito!

Reading this gave us such a smile! The tiny fruit diva reactions, the pomegranate love, the chaos, the laughter, all of it. We’re really happy to hear Fruit Golf could bring out that big-kid joy while still feeling welcoming, funny, and fun to share with others.

We also really appreciate you calling out the early access quirks with so much kindness and care. Feedback like this means a lot, and it helps us keep improving while holding onto the weird, chaotic heart of the game.

Thank you again for playing, for bringing friends and family into the mayhem, and for taking the time to write something so generous. <3

reviews.review_title: You'll have a love hate relationship.
reviews.review_description:
This game is a absolut blast. But u gota be quick if u slow down it becomes chaos. You're friends just straight bully u. But if u can. Speed run they ain't got time to mess u up.
This game has definitely made me have a love hate relationship now with my friends so be warned 😆 

One thing I whant to mention is the eatch ball has there own perks well mainly like stats so for example the watermelon is big and strong so if u hot like a sword that was placed buy a player it breaks the sword right away.

One other cool thing that i really love with this game is mobile players can play as well (vr vs mobile)

developer_response.body: Hey there! We appreciate you leaving a review. Speed running is a tactic I have used many times to avoid getting absolutely ganged up on by the saboteurs. Thanks for highlighting that each fruit has its own physics and abilities. The watermelon is definitely our strongest fruit and can hold its own against certain obstacles. We hope you keep swinging into the chaos!

reviews.review_description: This is a up to four person party game where one person golfs and the others are sabators placing obsticals on the couse to sabotage the golfer, once that player completes the couse (it is a one hole course so the turns are pretty quick), the next VR player becomes a golfer, the match is three rounds on three different courses the one with the least strokes wins.  Dont have fours friends with headsets, this game allow for a free phone/tablet companion app in either Android or IOS, the phone player can only be a sabator not a golfer.  This would be a great local party game with pass the headset.  There are I believe 6 different courses with three levels each, plenty of unlockable clubs, balls, and cosmetics for your avatar.  Don't expect true to life golfing in this game and you won't disapointed, the ball is a piece of fruit after all, each ftuit moves and bounces differently.  It is a fast paced chaotic fun party game.

developer_response.body: Hi Ellehcim99! We ap-peach-iate you leaving a review! We're happy to hear you enjoyed Fruit Golf and got to try golfing with the different clubs and fruits. Be sure to join us on Discord to unlock other exclusive content!

reviews.review_description: I don't really think there is a better golfing app on the market right now. The level of comedy and uniqueness that this game brings to the table is quite astounding. That being said of course there are glitches here and there but the dev team is pretty good about keeping in touch with their player base so things get ironed out pretty quickly all things considered. So if you're looking for a fun game to mess around with your friends, or if youve really just wanted to be "THAT GUY" (or girl) who has always wanted to mess up your buddies golf game, this might be the game for you!

developer_response.body:
Hi there, XAltMusic! We’re really glad the humor and chaos of Fruit Golf landed for you. We also appreciate your patience with the bugs and your support as we keep improving the game!

Really, thanks again for playing... and for embracing the chaos with your friends.

reviews.review_description:
I received a copy for testing, and I honestly haven't even had a chance to properly try out the mobile app so far.

That being said, I think the game was a blast to play with some big caveats.  The first is that there are no public lobbies, or even ways to see what private lobbies are currently going.  (You will need to have the players in mind ahead of time and join a specific room name together.)  Another big caveat is that the physics didn't feel realistic to me, as a lemon and a watermelon seemed to have the same level of heft and randomness of physics reactions to my putter.  I also got quite frustrated with how I would accidentally hit my ball backwards while trying to deal with obstacles or set up for a new shot.  The movement system was also quite frustrating and often sent me past where the ball was or angled me in a weird way.  You're also limited to only 4 players.  Also, a deterrent was the major bugs that will hopefully be worked out in the future--one of them was so game-breaking, we had to make a new lobby.  

Despite the many frustrations, I do think the game was really chaotic and enjoyable and I could see playing this with friends/family for a game night.  The fact that there's a free app to play along is a nice idea, though I don't honestly know how good the experience is (and assume the VR is better).  Looking forward to watching this game grow and updating my review accordingly.

developer_response.body:
Hi AroraMew! Thanks so much for the thoughtful review and for spending time with the game.

We really appreciate you calling out both the fun and the frustrating parts. It’s great to hear the chaotic game night feel came through, but the issues you mentioned around lobbies, movement, ball handling, and bugs are all fair notes, and they’re SO helpful for us to hear.

Sorry you ran into a bug bad enough to force a new lobby... that’s not a great experience.

We’re still actively improving Fruit Golf, so thanks again for the honest feedback and for giving it a shot!

reviews.review_description:
Fruit Golf is the kind of game that turns a normal hangout into absolute nonsense in the best way possible. The asymmetrical setup with one player in VR putting and others using the mobile companion app, or also in VR as saboteurs, works surprisingly well, and the saboteur mechanics are where the magic happens. Watching your friends unleash ridiculous obstacles while you’re trying to line up a shot with a screaming piece of fruit is pure comedy. The courses are creative, the hazards are unpredictable, and the reactions you get from everyone in the room make this feel less like mini golf and more like a party game disguised as one. 

That said, the movement system needs some tuning. Teleport locomotion works, but it can drop you into awkward positions around the ball, sometimes facing completely away from the hole, which breaks the flow. You can use snap turning to get into the proper position but it takes up time, and by then, you’re surrounded by obstacles. You can use your putter to knock the obstacles out of the way though. It also would benefit from public lobbies since right now you need to already have people to play with, up to four players, which is the perfect number to balance golfer vs saboteurs. 

Even with those issues, the core experience is strong enough to carry it. It is weird, loud, and consistently funny, and absolutely worth playing if you have a group ready to cause chaos. I will definitely make this a five star review if the issues are addressed and improved.

developer_response.body:
Hi MissChiefVR! Thank you so much for this thoughtful review and for taking the time to really dig into both what’s working and what still needs improvement.

We’re really happy to hear the chaos, comedy, and party game energy came through so strongly. We also really appreciate the honest notes about movement and lobby flow.

Thanks again for playing, for your support, and for sharing such generous and thoughtful feedback. It is *berry* appreciated.

reviews.review_title: Great Golf game with friends and family
reviews.review_description: Had loads of fun as the Golfer tested out different fruits balls and clubs in the game that you unloxk from playing loved them all. The pirate map was super fun with the shark! Saboteur was trying to block me off from getting to the goal they managed abit but got to the goal in the end! Love that people can join in and play the game on the phones as Saboteur if they don't have a headset to have great time playing the game. Would recommend the game to any that likes fast paced golf game :)

developer_response.body: Hey Warmarck! Thank you cherry much for the kind review. We’re glad you enjoyed golfing with all of the fruit, including the unlockable ones. The Pirate courses are definitely a team favorite, and we’re happy to hear you could use Stu the shark to your advantage. Making VR more accessible is what we’re all about, so we’re happy to hear that you loved the option to have mobile saboteurs join in on the fun.

reviews.review_description: Here you do different things on golf course and add obstacles to player game. Also you could golf in this game too. Courses are great and fun. Mechanics and controls work perfectly. Really would play this game over and over with friends

developer_response.body: Hey GMsupr! We appreciate you leaving a review. We're glad you enjoyed the courses and controls. We hope you and your friends continue to have the berry best time!

reviews.review_description:
I had a blast playing this game with a party of four. You play either as a golfer or a sabotager. As a golfer, your goal is to get your ball to the hole in as few tries as possible, while the sabotager’s job is—just as the name suggests—to stop you from reaching the goal by throwing different objects onto the ground as obstacles.
The game is really fun, and you can dodge traps dropped by the sabotagers. You can also talk with your party, which makes everything even more enjoyable. They made the game hilarious and exciting, and I really enjoyed it.
Highly recommend!

developer_response.body: Hiya DeafyPro! Thanks for the review. Chatting to friends while playing Fruit Golf definitely makes for a more fun (and competitive) experience. We’re so glad you had a blast playing multiplayer, and we hope you continue to swing and sabotage your way to victory!

reviews.review_description:
This was a chaotic and fun time.  You have a bunch of nice putt putt courses to play through with a nice twist.  Instead of balls your using fruit.  Each has its own unique characteristics to keep up with the obstacles thrown your way.  
  It's nice to have as it's not just pass and play the whole group can join in on their phones.  While one person golf's the rest have the job to drop obstacles to impede the golfers path.  These can range from sharks to explosive barrels.  
   No matter what position your in for a good laugh.  You even have the sabotours cheering you on when you make a good shot over the obstacles.  
   While the gameplay is fantastic it has a few issues.  One of my favorite parts is the customization of the characters.  So many options to unlock with a variety of heads and outfits to match your crazy putters. Overall I think this is a must have for anyone looking for a fun party game to play with family and friends.

developer_response.body: Thanks a bunch for the review, TbcVRgaming. We’re so happy you were able to experience each fruit and their physics. Our team worked hard to design unique obstacles for the saboteurs. The various explosive obstacles are a personal favourite, so I am glad you had a blast using them! We are super passionate about creating playful and social experiences, and we’re hyped to hear your group had a good laugh. We hope Fruit Golf this remains a game night staple!

reviews.review_description:
A very unique party game that puts a fun spin on regular mini golf that's perfect for the casual gamer!

It will be a go-to title for me to play with family members at family gatherings – with its pick-up-and-play nature that even non-VR players can understand within minutes


One player plays mini golf, over themed courses that have bags of character. Whilst the other players do their very best to make it as difficult as possible by placing traps around the course.


The game works excellently whenever everyone is playing on VR headsets, with the only drawback being it doesn't have public lobbies. -  (maybe in a future update?)


But the twist is that players can also download the free app to sabotage their friends' attempts to golf if they don't own the game/have a VR headset.

It doesn't work as seamlessly as if everyone are on a VR headset (where there can be minor audio hitches with the voice chat).

And phone players can only sabotage and not golf...but after hearing friends curse at your traps, you will want to pick up the game to be the golfer yourself!


As a local multiplayer, it really shines, as you can pass the headset around to take turns, and the audio issues on the phone app don't matter (when you're all in the same room).


A great buy for anyone that is looking for a chaotic laugh-out multiplayer game that you can play at family gatherings.

developer_response.body: Hey, Burntbreadman! Thanks for taking the time to leave a review. It means a lot to us. Thank you for highlighting that Fruit Golf is easy to pick up and play. I’m sure that will be extra a-peel-ing for those who are new to VR and want to swing into the chaos. Fruit Golf was made with family game nights in mind, and we’re glad you had a great time with our pass-and-play mode. We appreciate you calling out public lobbies and voice chat. Feedback like that is super valuable as we’re always looking for ways that we can improve. Thanks a bunch, and we hope your next round is the zest!

reviews.review_description: Perfect for a games night can even get players in on the app . Very unique take on mini golf a lot of fun

developer_response.body:
Thanks 🍌a bunch🍌, TipsySonic! We appreciate the review SO much! Please let us know in the Discord if we can ever help you with anything! 

https://discord.com/invite/3j2R2XMxFC

reviews.review_description: This game is so fun to play just with even with strangers or friends and being able to have a challenge￼. It was very fun with even the people I was playing with. ￼

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you, RealSupervigy! It means a lot to hear you had fun playing with both friends and strangers. Creating that kind of chaotic, silly challenge was exactly what we were aiming for. Thanks for taking a swing on us and leaving a review!!!

reviews.review_description: Think of this game more of a party game than a golf game in VR. You can have friends join on their phone as well, so it makes it a great pick up and play game. There are unlockable cosmetics to customize both your character and your putter/ball. The courses are really fun and look great. There is no smooth turning nor natural movement, only snap and teleport, so keep that in mind if that matters to you. Great fun game to pick up and play with friends!

developer_response.body:
Hi there, RobbieArchitect!

Thank you **berry much** for your thoughtful review! We love that you picked up on the party game vibes of Fruit Golf... that’s exactly the kind of fun experience we were hoping to create. Being able to have friends jump in on their phones makes it extra sweet, and we’re glad you’re enjoying the courses and unlockable cosmetics too.

And thanks for calling out the locomotion options! That’s helpful for other players to know before they tee off. We’re always listening to feedback and looking at ways to keep improving the experience.

Really appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. Hope your next round is extra a-peel-ing!

reviews.review_description:
To be honnest, this game is funny for one or two games but some times saboter can be boring. The methods of moving are not my type and some times after moving you might have to change your putter hand...
Funny to play with friends, you can use the app as well with 1 quest device and switch to be able to play and you can sabotage with your phones ;)

developer_response.body:
Hey IMisterlight! Thanks a *bunch* for giving Fruit Golf a try and for sharing your thoughts. We totally get that not every game is everyone’s cup of fruit juice. We're glad you found it fun with friends though!

We’re always listening to feedback and looking for ways to keep things fresh and enjoyable for all players. And good catch on the hand swap after moving! That's helpful feedback for our team as we continue polishing things. We're also hoping to explore more methods of movement, so stay tuned to potential new betas to try!

Thanks again for playing and taking the time to leave a review. We appreciate it **berry much!**

reviews.review_title: A Golf Game Built for Laughs
reviews.review_description: I played Fruit Golf VR with friends using one headset and phone sabotage, and it was pure chaos from the first swing. Trying to line up a shot while sharks flopped onto the course and portals opened under the ball had everyone yelling and laughing. Each fruit really does feel different; learning how one bends physics can turn a bad shot into a miracle, if no one ruins it first. The courses are wild, jumping from underwater to space to a kitchen counter, and the sabotage tools make every turn unpredictable. It’s not about skill as much as timing and cruelty, which fits the tone perfectly. Solo play is fun for learning the physics, but the game shines brightest with people trying to destroy you.

developer_response.body:
Hi there Fearian! We love that the sharks, portals, and sabotage chaos had everyone yelling and laughing. (When we playtest, our team laughs and yells too!)

You nailed it too: sometimes it’s less about skill and more about perfectly timed fruity cruelty. Thanks for playing and sharing the fun with friends!

reviews.review_description:
We love Acron with the kids, I just found this game and had big hopes. Unfortunatelly it's a disaster in multiplayer.
1. My daughter's ipad was unable to find the app
2. My S22 wasn't able to collect some floor data of my room
3. We have managed to play with 2 android phones and 1 headset but it was a disaster: choppy, laggy, frustrating.
After 4 rounds I refund this game (my daughter quit after round 3).

developer_response.body:
Hi Sztrovacsek81,

We love Acron, too!

We're terribly sorry to hear that you had several technical issues in multiplayer. We’re currently in Early Access, and this feedback plays a huge role. We’re especially interested in hearing about your experience. 

Please write us at support@fruitgolf.com, or join our Discord (fruitgolf.com/discord) and we can troubleshoot through this together! We’d genuinely love the chance to look into what happened with your setup.

In the meantime, these tips might help:

- As a different way to find the app on the Apple Store, try using the link on www.fruitgolf.com.

- Playing in a bright, well-lit room can help the headset finish room setup more reliably.

- Making sure all devices are on the same, stable Wi-Fi can reduce lag or stuttering.

- Double-checking that each device is on the latest system update.

We understand you’ve already chosen to request a refund, and we truly appreciate you giving the game a try. If you ever decide to revisit it in the future, or if you’re open to sharing a bit more about your setup, we’d be happy to help and look into what may have gone wrong!

reviews.review_description: Fruit Golf is one of those VR games that just clicks the moment you’re playing with a group. I had an absolute blast and honestly couldn’t stop laughing. The mix of golfing and sabotage is chaos in the best way, especially the first time I dropped a trap and watched it blow up someone’s ball, the reaction from everyone was priceless and I knew right then this game was going to be a good one. The fruit physics are a great touch too, I didn’t expect them to actually roll differently, but once I realized they do it made everything funnier and more unpredictable. The visuals and audio are spot on, and the overall pacing is fast, fun, and super replayable. I easily spent close to two hours in game without realizing it. Movement could use a little polishing since smooth turning made me pretty dizzy and I ended up sticking with snap turning, and navigating as the saboteur felt a bit clunky at times, but nothing that took away from how much fun the experience is overall. Easy 5 stars from me.

developer_response.body: Hi Angelfire340! Thanks a bunch for leaving such a thoughtful review. This made our day. We're super passionate about making fun and social VR experiences, and it brings us so much joy to hear how much fun you had sabotaging your friends. We appreciate the feedback regarding the movement. We definitely want Fruit Golf to be as smooth of an experience  as possible, so this is super helpful. You're the zest and we hope the next time you play is as chaotic as the last!

reviews.review_description: This is an interesting mini golf game where one person is trying to complete a course while everyone else is trying to sabotage them using a variety of obstacles that get in their way. This is a very unique game and it puts an interesting twist on mini golf. There are still many bugs that need to be worked out, including locomotion issues and server issues. This game has a lot of potential and will be a great game in time after some more polish.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review, Tyler905. We're glad you enjoyed our chaotic twist on mini golf. We appreciate you calling out the locomotion options. We are constantly looking for ways to make Fruit Golf a better experience, so feedback like this is super valuable. Hopefully we can address these issues and you’ll tee off with us again.

reviews.review_description: This is more of a party game than a traditional golf sim. You don't need precise controls or swing angles. Just guide the fruits to the goal while dodging traps and obstacles! It's fun because each fruit ball has its own unique personality and shape. The lemon is such a coward that I feel bad hitting it with the club lol.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, HaaN_Jabs! Glad you were digging Fruit Golf's party game vibes. We feel you though 😭🍋 the lemon has been through so much!

reviews.review_description:
We all love Walkabout Minigolf, but this is something entirely different because it allows you to mess with your opponent! We've all wanted to do this. The addition of a mobile app is very clever, pulling from games like Acron. However, the mobile app needs some work. Thankfully, that app is free.
Once some of the lobby bugs get worked out and mobile is fixed, it will be an awesome way to have fun with friends. Have some faith, the team is hard at work collecting feedback and making changes, (I'm one of many helping give feedback) so supporting them would certainly help bring this to life.

developer_response.body: Hey there, SaltyDadda. Thanks for checking out Fruit Golf and for leaving a review. Thanks as well for pointing out the bugs and the feedback regarding our mobile app. Early Access feedback like this really helps us keep polishing the game.

reviews.review_description: Who doesn't love chaos and mayhem? This is no ordinary golf game. You play as a golfer and you have three other saboteurs trying to stop you from getting to the goal. And the goal is not even easy to get to, because guess what? The goal is against you as well. When you play as a golfer, it's a challenging golfing game that leaves no moment for you to relax. When playing as a saboteur, it's a lot of fun making the golfer's day worse and worse. With deployable obstacles and careful planning, you could make that golfer rage quit. I like the mechanics and the customization of the game. The pacing is also nice. If there is anything to improve, it is the customizability of the game sessions, the ability to control everything. The more options we have, the better.

developer_response.body: Hey Majed6! We love chaos and mayhem 🙋 Thanks so much for leaving us a review. We're happy to hear you enjoyed all of the customizability that Fruit Golf has to offer. We hope you continue to make swing into the chaos and ruin the golfer's day as a saboteur!

reviews.review_description: They combined mini golf with the artist feel of Mario Kart!  I love the feeling of escape it provides, too.  My daughter and I are currently taking turns playing this and trying to beat each other's scores.  She's currently winning, but I fully intend to practice when she isn't around.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review, Mcghilton! Families that game together, stay together! If you have any feedback, we're happy to hear! Just join our Discord here: https://discord.com/invite/3j2R2XMxFC

reviews.review_description: As an avid Walkabout Mini golf player I was very excited to play Fruit Golf.  As far as the golf goes it's ok, Walkabout has much better physics however the obstacles you can sabotage players with are immensely fun to use and play through!  There are too many to list here, but just know they can block you, move you, and obstruct you in so many ways.  This is balanced however, because you can also hit them with the club and they'll disappear, so you don't have to golf through them if you hit them fast enough to clear them out.  Lots of levels and different clubs to play with, overall very fun game!

developer_response.body:
Hi EpiXan! Thanks a bunch for giving Fruit Golf a swing and for the thoughtful comparison! We're huge fans of Walkabout too!

We’re glad you had fun causing a little sabotage chaos! We appreciate you playing and sharing your review!

reviews.review_title: Great in VR, not so great on the phone
reviews.review_description:
VR play works very well for the most part with only some mildly frustrating movement and camera issues, which could be easily solved with some QOL fixes. The obstacles are extremely fun and feel really well balanced, the maps are visually pleasing, the sound design is superb, with the fruit 'golf balls' providing much more depth than I originally imagined they would.
The phone app (tested on S25 Ultra) was an unexpectedly poor experience. Awful camera controls and visual clipping issues, along with poor lobby/party visual feedback, make for a terrible experience.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful feedback, iGetDenied. We’re really glad you enjoyed the VR experience -- especially the obstacles, maps, sound design, and the different fruit balls. 

We also appreciate you calling out the issues you ran into on the phone companion. That’s helpful for us as we keep improving the experience, especially around camera controls and lobby feedback. We will be sure to take a look at the S25 line for sure. 

Thanks again for giving Fruit Golf a swing!

reviews.review_description: This i found to be a great game, with only a few complaints, though only minor ones. Firstly, lets start with the many positives. This game has a good amount of maps with creative props for each, and several balls each with different properties. this allows a lot of replayability, from learning how to use a new ball to make a new playstyle, to trying all the maps. Another positive is how you can use each prop, from having anchors which can block balls from going anywhere, to dynamite, sending balls to the skies, to even portals which can send balls from the end to the start, putting you in the lead. I tried both single and multiplayer on this game and both have solid gameplay, with singleplayer having bot controlled prop placements which seem almost human like with their placements, and seamless gameplay. Multiplayer has a few bugs, but overall is amazingly done, with each player getting a chance to use each prop at least once. I would also like to bring up the balls as another positive, with each having their own stats and ways of bouncing around the course. Personally I preferred the watermelon, which was able to travel quite well and bounce amazingly. For the negatives I only have to complain about a few bugs that i ran into, and the can on the kitchen course. The bugs mainly revolved around hosts leaving or having issues I believe, but they seem easy to fix. The can was a personal one, I did not like how i couldn't see where to hit the ball, and how weirdly it got sent when it went through. Otherwise this was a great game, and definitely worth checking out and getting. 4/5, only for the bugs and can, would recommend to anyone who wants a fun party game to play.

developer_response.body:
Yeah, Xan9! Go Team Watermelon! 🍉

It’s awesome hearing you experimented with the different balls and sabotage props. Thanks as well for pointing out the bugs and the kitchen can feedback. Early Access feedback like this really helps us keep polishing the game.

Appreciate you playing and recommending Fruit Golf! Hope your next round is extra juicy.

reviews.review_description:
Fruit Golf is one in a melon 🍉 I’ve played other golf games, but this one was so unique and fun. I played multiplayer with friends on mobile and it was such a treat!! I thought it really captured the silly and absurd energy of competition among friends. 

You can tell that so much thought went into this game, from the clever names for the courses and cups to all the small details throughout the courses. I can’t wait to see what other cups they’ll come out with 💛

developer_response.body: Thank you for the coolest review, CoolPisces! We love all the fun details the amazing team put in as well!

reviews.review_title: Great for families and friend groups!
reviews.review_description: Fruit Golf is just plain fun. Super easy to jump into, totally chaotic in the best way, and somehow even more hilarious when things go wrong. Playing in VR with friends is awesome, and the fact that others can join in from their phones makes it even better. Great for family nights. We’ve had some big laughs.

developer_response.body:
Sounds like your rounds got delightfully out of hand... just how we like it! 😈

We’re so glad Fruit Golf brought the chaos and big laughs to your family nights. Thank you so much for playing and giving us a review, dnplayaone.

reviews.review_description:
Fruit Golf is one of the most unexpectedly DELIGHTFULLY fun games I’ve played in VR. It combines the relaxing vibe of mini golf with the absurd, juicy chaos of smashing fruit – it's the perfect mash up!

The environments are colorful and really pop (I keep noticing new details every time I play), great humor throughout,  and the gameplay is easy to jump into but hard to put down. This game was MADE for Friday night with friends...lots of crazy chaos and trash talking. We were going to play one course just to check it out and ended up playing well past 30 minutes.

This will be my new go-to when bringing my non-VR friends into the game.  It's casual, social, and seriously fun.

Add Fruit Golf to your cart – it’s the freshest game in the VR aisle!  🍉⛳️

developer_response.body:
Hi GypsyyKris! This review is the juiciest! 

We’re so happy Fruit Golf delivered the kind of chaotic Friday-night fun we were hoping for. Hearing that you kept playing “just one more round” is the sweetest compliment.

Thanks for the kind words. See you on the course!

reviews.review_description:
Ran on Meta Quest 3 and the idea of using different fruits as golf balls, each with unique attributes and physics, is both creative and hilarious. It’s lighthearted fun that’s perfect for kids too – they’ll love the colorful visuals and playful mechanics (+ customization!)

The game throws all kinds of wacky obstacles in your path, and the ability for friends to sabotage your progress adds a chaotic, competitive edge that keeps things exciting. I also really appreciate the companion app, which lets a friend join the action from mobile – that's always a nice touch!

Even in Early Access, Fruit Golf is already a fun experience and I’m excited to see where the devs take it next. Love the pineapple western combo personally (and I love pineapples on a pizza!) 😉

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! Glad to hear you got to enjoy our companion app too (and we also love pineapples on a pizza 🍍)

reviews.review_description:
I've played this game a whole bunch with my family and a few friends on my husband's account. Our kids have a great time messing with the golfer, and the way we can work together while sabotaging can be satisfying when it works out or absolutely hilarious when it fails. We always end up yelling and laughing and no one ever feels left out (which has been a problem for us with other games on oculus...). The app is intuitive and can be given to the young ones to be included if VR is still too much for them. Price is really good for a silly fun night or two. 

Also, the noises and expressions that the different fruit make are so hilarious! You'll understand when you look down before shooting :)

developer_response.body:
Hiya Black_Bean_Pirate!

This is exactly the kind of fruity chaos we love hearing about. Kids teaming up to sabotage the golfer, everyone yelling and laughing, and nobody left out. 🥹

We’re also very happy the phone app helped get the younger players in on the fun. And yes… those fruit noises and expressions definitely have our team cracking up too.

Thanks a bunch for playing with the whole crew and sharing the laughs!

reviews.review_description:
A good early access.I had a god time with the two different courses. The environments are nice.  The game is challeging to get the fruit to its destination, that makes it fun. It has good customization for characters and balls. Could be cool to be able to customize your golf club as well and maybe having some power ups when striking the ball. 
Sometimes the saboteur obstacles can be too much. There was one run when plenty of traps were place in the same spot, totally blocking the path.
Additionally, when hitting obstacles with the club, some of these could had a more feedback in having some extra particles.
Also, it would be nice if the course itself has obstacles or advantages on its own. In the first course, it would be nice to use one of those cannons to be able to move foward.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! Thanks so much for your review and for your kind words. We're so glad you had a good time and enjoyed customizing your experience with the different fruit . You can also unlock unique golf clubs by  doing challenges. 

We also really appreciate the feedback. We're constantly looking to improve the balance of the game in terms of obstacle rates! And we totally hear you regarding course specific obstacles and our next course will have elements you can interact with, so stay tuned :)

reviews.review_description: You need to rewire your head as this is not the crazy golf game you are used to. It is trying very hard to be different, which is commendable. I'll be very curious to see how it evolves.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing! We hope you stick around because we'll have more chaos arriving to Fruit Golf soon

reviews.review_description: Truly fun for all! ❤️ have a blast strategizing what flavors suit your style, sabotage your friends, or just relax and hang in this adorable putt paradise! 🍍🍉🍋

developer_response.body:
A zesty review deserves a zesty thank you, ChappellRoan! 🍋

We’re so happy you’re enjoying the fruity strategy, sabotage shenanigans, and relaxing vibes of our little putting paradise. Thanks for playing and spreading the love!

With sabotage flying everywhere, all we can say is “Good luck, babe!”

reviews.review_description: A fun casual way to smack fruit across the fairway with friends! Recommend to any fruit lover (or hater)…🍐🫐🍋🍎🍍

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review. The fruit haters deserve representation too!

reviews.review_title: A fresh take on crazy mini-golf
reviews.review_description: The themed courses in Fruit Golf are so much fun and I can't wait to keep playing with friends and sabotage their runs!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing! Keep on sabotaging
